Allied commandos (Gregory Peck, David Niven, Anthony Quinn) try to knock out a Nazi fortress over the Aegean.
programme.title: The Guns of Navarone

programme.desc: Cartoons
programme.title: Cartoons

programme.desc: S01 E20 — Blondie's Double Dagwood thinks Blondie is singing at a nightclub, after Mr. Dithers sees a woman who looks like her.
programme.title: Blondie

programme.desc: The queen of Egypt (Elizabeth Taylor) seduces Julius Caesar (Rex Harrison), but when he is killed, she uses Mark Antony (Richard Burton) as her new protector.
programme.title: Cleopatra

programme.desc: A modern-day blacksmith becomes the toast of King Arthur's court after being transported to sixth-century England.
programme.title: A Connecticut Yankee in King Arthur's Court

programme.desc: Superspy Derek Flint (James Coburn), agent of Z.O.W.I.E., stops a geothermal plot to control the world's weather.
programme.title: Our Man Flint

programme.desc: U.S. soldiers (Van Johnson, John Hodiak, Ricardo Montalban) from all over fight in the Battle of the Bulge.
programme.title: Battleground

programme.desc: A Greek partisan (Telly Savalas) helps Allied prisoners escape with ancient art from a German major's (Roger Moore) island prison.
programme.title: Escape to Athena

programme.desc: A reporter hears how an ex-convict (James Caan) and an Indian woman (Stefanie Powers) destroyed an Old West town overnight.
programme.title: Gone With the West

programme.desc: The Missing Diplomat Story Steve travels to Barcelona to retrieve records of top-secret conversations from a European diplomat who has disappeared.
programme.title: Dangerous Assignment

programme.desc: S02 E10 — The Picture War Mike gathers evidence on a shady nightclub owner.
programme.title: Man With a Camera

programme.desc: S01 E20 — Blondie's Double Dagwood thinks Blondie is singing at a nightclub, after Mr. Dithers sees a woman who looks like her.
programme.title: Blondie

programme.desc: S05 E17 — Main Street U.S.A. Lucy goes to the aid of a small town in a war against freeways.
programme.title: The Lucy Show

programme.desc: S02 E14 — The Ape Adam and Little Joe warn Hoss when he tries to help Arnie, a man with the mind of a child and an uncontrollable temper.
programme.title: Bonanza

programme.desc: A librarian hears a sour note when a charming rogue convinces Iowa townspeople to start a boys marching band.
programme.title: The Music Man

programme.desc: A group of Allied soldiers are intent on breaking out, not only to escape, but also to draw Nazi forces away from battle to search for fugitives. Among the prisoners are American Captain Virgil Hilts and British Squadron Leader Roger Bartlett.
programme.title: The Great Escape

programme.desc: Ersatz kryptonite laced with tobacco tar splits Superman (Christopher Reeve) in two: good Clark Kent and bad Man of Steel.
programme.title: Superman III

programme.desc: Mistaken identity spurs a foreign spy (James Mason) to pursue an innocent New Yorker (Cary Grant), all the way to Mount Rushmore.
programme.title: North by Northwest

programme.desc: A Supreme Court justice on a duck-hunting foray becomes involved in serious trouble with the citizens in a small town.
programme.title: A Stranger in Town

programme.desc: The nation's capital faces a bizarre political scandal when a top presidential aide suffers a severe personality change.
programme.title: The Werewolf of Washington

programme.desc: S02 E14 — The Ape Adam and Little Joe warn Hoss when he tries to help Arnie, a man with the mind of a child and an uncontrollable temper.
programme.title: Bonanza

programme.desc: Two Montana cowboys (Jerry Reed, Tom Selleck) head for Nashville and help a woman (Morgan Fairchild) find her sister.
programme.title: The Concrete Cowboys

programme.desc: The nation's capital faces a bizarre political scandal when a top presidential aide suffers a severe personality change.
programme.title: The Werewolf of Washington

programme.desc: The arrival of wealthy bachelors in town causes an uproar when families with single daughters aggressively seek engagements, including the Bennet family, with five eligible daughters.
programme.title: Pride and Prejudice

programme.desc: A British gentleman's (Laurence Olivier) innocent bride (Joan Fontaine) grapples with intrusive reminders of his deceased wife.
programme.title: Rebecca

programme.desc: The Tabernacle Choir at Temple Square performs songs of hope, faith and inspiration; singing sacred hymns both new and old from around the world as well as uplifting selections from the American songbook.
programme.title: Music & the Spoken Word

programme.desc: Superspy Derek Flint (James Coburn), agent of Z.O.W.I.E., stops a geothermal plot to control the world's weather.
programme.title: Our Man Flint

programme.desc: A tracker (Sammy Davis Jr.) helps a white rancher (Ernest Borgnine) avenge his son and rescue his daughter.
programme.title: The Trackers

programme.desc: A lusty Greek peasant (Anthony Quinn) shows a British writer (Alan Bates) how to live and run a lignite mine.
programme.title: Zorba the Greek

programme.desc: A modern-day blacksmith becomes the toast of King Arthur's court after being transported to sixth-century England.
programme.title: A Connecticut Yankee in King Arthur's Court

programme.desc: A bounty hunter (Glenn Ford) faces his son's killers with help from the son (Michael Burns) of an outlaw he has killed.
programme.title: Santee

programme.desc: A clumsy young man nurtures a plant and discovers that it's carnivorous, forcing him to kill to feed it.
programme.title: The Little Shop of Horrors

programme.desc: John Adams (William Daniels), Benjamin Franklin (Howard da Silva) and Thomas Jefferson (Ken Howard) lead the way to the Colonies' Declaration of Independence.
Three retired Texas Rangers rescue a former colleague and eventually become the sheriff and deputies of Waco.
programme.title: The Over-the-Hill Gang Rides Again

programme.desc: A reporter writes a fictitious column about a man named John Doe, who claims to despair at America's neglect of the little people and plans to kill himself. To maintain the ruse, the newspaper hires a ballplayer-turned-hobo to pose as John Doe.
programme.title: Meet John Doe

programme.desc: A teenager (Tuesday Weld) wants to wear a strapless gown to the prom.
programme.title: Rock, Rock, Rock!

programme.desc: A stewardess (Doris Day) runs for her life once she realizes her second husband (Louis Jourdan) killed her first.
programme.title: Julie

programme.desc: Little Alice (Fiona Fullerton) falls into a strange land and meets the White Rabbit (Michael Crawford), March Hare (Peter Sellers) and other odd characters out of Lewis Carroll's story.
programme.title: Alice's Adventures in Wonderland

programme.desc: Dorothy tries to save her dog from a storm and eases on down the road to see the Wiz with Tin Man, Scarecrow and Cowardly Lion.
programme.title: The Wiz

programme.desc: A lawyer (Ginger Rogers) bails out and then marries a Hollywood tough guy (Jack Carson) who's a milquetoast in real life.
programme.title: The Groom Wore Spurs

programme.desc: An armchair explorer (Bob Hope), a G-woman (Edie Adams), and a Soviet spy (Anita Ekberg) and her colleague go on safari for a lost moon capsule.
programme.title: Call Me Bwana

S01 E07 — A Picture From Hell Talia and Nimrod enjoy intimate time in their bedroom after the kids leave the house; Dana's relationship with Dr. Ostrovsky becomes more intense; Hazav prepares for his pre-Army tests.
programme.title: Prisoners of War

programme.desc: S01 E08 — Family Portrait Uri and Nimrod disappear, and their families are starting to get worried; the Klein family is finding a few joyous moments, precisely when Nimrod is not there; Talia shows Nimrod the injuries she got while he had nightmares.
programme.title: Prisoners of War

programme.desc: The Wounded Warrior Project helps wounded service members returning home with mental health, career counseling, and long-term rehabilitative care, as well as advocates for wounded warriors and their families.
programme.title: Wounded Warriors Project

programme.desc: Adaeze connects with her Nigerian heritage, and Lenny experiments with sweet cream and kola nut for a new customer's surprise engagement party.
programme.title: It's a Sweet World

programme.desc: S01 E75 Tel Hai, Moroccan Jewry, Israel's Nuclear Whistleblower Unpacking the history of the 1920 Battle of Tel Hai, Moroccan Jewry and their almost complete departure from this North African nation, and Israeli Nuclear Whistleblower, Mordechai Vanunu.
programme.title: Unpacked

programme.desc: A 4th of July Shabbat The greatest Jewish musicians from around the nation salute the United States of America with a special Shabbat on this American Independence Day.
programme.title: JLTV's Friday Night Shabbat Services

programme.desc: Since the founding of the United States, Jewish-American patriots courageously serve their country; features archival footage and personal testimonies of war veterans from World War II, Korea, Vietnam and Iraq.
programme.title: Jewish-American Patriots

programme.desc: S01 E01 What did George Washington believe about religion? Why did Washington write an official letter to a small Jewish community in 1790? How is Washington's letter relevant in the 21st century? Addressing these challenging questions.
programme.title: To Bigotry No Sanction

programme.title: To Bigotry No Sanction

programme.desc: S01 E09 — Awake at Night Nimrod still suffers from nightmares and dreams of the night he killed Amiel. Yael tries to find Ilan's home address and decides to go there without his knowledge.
programme.title: Prisoners of War

programme.desc: S01 E10 — The Tape Talia makes Nimrod choose between a future with her, or a life of misery; Nurit has to choose between Yaki and Uri; Yael has to confront the death of her brother.
programme.title: Prisoners of War

programme.desc: Chair The secret word is chair; guests hail from Los Angeles, New York, Scotland and Virginia; an uncooperative pet bird.
programme.title: You Bet Your Life

programme.title: You Bet Your Life

programme.desc: S03 E03 — Israeli Food Israeli food is a cultural experience; singing of all foods Israeli, such as falafel, shakshuka and hummus; Hebrew Word of the Day: Ochel or food; instrument of the day: Mandolin.
programme.title: Rhythm 'N Ruach

programme.desc: S03 E04 — Israel Defense Force The protectors of the Jewish homeland, the Israel Defense Forces or IDF is part of every Israeli life; singing songs of peace, resilience, and inner strength; Hebrew Word of the Day: Tzahal or IDF; instrument of the day: Electric Guitar.
programme.title: Rhythm 'N Ruach
